May 13, 2026

Dear Hiring Manager,

I am applying for the Senior Platform Engineer role on your Developer
Experience team. My recent work has focused on turning scattered operational
workflows into dependable internal tools: I led a migration from hand-run
release scripts to an observable deployment pipeline, reduced flaky rollback
steps by 38%, and wrote the runbooks that helped support engineers diagnose
customer-impacting incidents without waiting on the platform team.

What drew me to this role is the mix of product empathy and infrastructure
discipline. In my last role, I partnered with design, security, and recruiting
operations to replace a brittle intake form with a typed approval workflow. The
project was not just a backend migration; it included accessibility reviews,
clear error states, and training docs for non-technical users. That combination
matches the way your team describes developer tools as products, not just
systems.

I would bring:

- TypeScript, Node.js, PostgreSQL, and Kubernetes experience across
  user-facing and internal platforms.
- A habit of writing migration plans that include rollback paths, audit
  logging, and stakeholder communication.
- Comfort translating ambiguous asks from support, recruiting, and product
  teams into maintainable engineering work.

Thank you for considering my application. I would welcome the chance to talk
about how I can help your team make everyday engineering workflows faster,
safer, and easier to trust.

Sincerely,

Maya Chen
